raw_text:
All searches are complete. I have strong evidence for two of the three variances and no context whatsoever for Subscriptions. Here is the full commentary.

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

February 2026 produced three significant variances against budget. Advertising & Marketing overspent by £667 (+66.7%) due to a deliberate, MD-approved pull-forward of the Q2 spring campaign from March into February, confirmed by both email approval and calendar evidence. Repairs & Maintenance exceeded budget by £786 (+786.3%), driven entirely by an emergency HVAC failure in the server room on 14 February; the £886 repair was CFO-approved on the same day and is a one-off, non-recurring event. Subscriptions came in at £0 against a £1,200 budget (-100.0%), representing a full underspend; no supporting context was found across any source and this line requires immediate review to determine whether invoices are delayed, contracts have been cancelled, or a coding issue exists. Taken together, the two overspends are well-evidenced and approved; the Subscriptions underspend is the primary area of uncertainty requiring follow-up.

---

## LINE COMMENTARY

---

**Advertising & Marketing | +£667 (+66.7%) | Budget: £1,000 | Actual: £1,667**

Actuals exceeded budget by £667, driven by the deliberate pull-forward of the Q2 spring campaign from its originally planned March timing into February. The decision was taken at agency recommendation, citing a weather-sensitive launch window, and was formally approved by the MD via email on 1 February 2026. Slack channel records from 3 February confirm the campaign team acknowledged the reforecast commitment of approximately £1,600 against the £1,000 March plan, and calendar evidence confirms the campaign went live on 5 February with search and social channels activated. A budget reforecast note was attached to the MD approval email. The variance is expected to self-correct over the two-month window (February–March) as the March budget will see a corresponding underspend.

Sources:
- Slack - 2024-11-23T12:15:00Z - slack-advertising_&_marketing-broad - "marketing-slack-2026-02-03: Campaign team — moving the Q2 spring campaign launch to this month. Client approval came in early, let's capitalise. Total Feb commitment will be ~£1,600 vs the £1,000 March plan. Approvals from MD signed off."
- Gmail - 2024-11-23T18:15:00Z - gmail-advertising_&_marketing-broad - "gmail-2026-02-01-campaign-approval: MD approval for Q2 campaign acceleration. Spring campaign moved from March to February at agency recommendation — weather-sensitive launch window. Budget reforecast note attached."
- Calendar - 2024-11-23T22:45:00Z - calendar-advertising_&_marketing-narrow - "cal-2026-02-05: Campaign live — search and social activated. Digital agency confirmed all creatives approved."

---

**Repairs Maintenance | +£786 (+786.3%) | Budget: £100 | Actual: £886**

Actuals exceeded budget by £786, representing an 886% overspend on a normally low-activity line. The variance is fully attributable to a single emergency event: the overnight failure of the HVAC unit in the server room on 14 February 2026. An emergency engineer was called by the building manager, and FastAir Engineering issued an invoice of £886.30 inclusive of parts and an emergency call-out rate. The CFO approved both the quote and the invoice on the same day. Slack records confirm the urgency of the situation and the same-day CFO sign-off. This is a non-recurring, unplanned expenditure; no budget provision was in place for emergency HVAC works, which explains the full budget absorption. No further spend on this line is anticipated in connection with this incident.

Sources:
- Slack - 2024-11-19T12:15:00Z - slack-repairs_maintenance-broad - "ops-slack-2026-02-14: URGENT — HVAC unit in server room failed overnight. Building manager called emergency engineer. Quote came in at £886. CFO approved. Works completed today."
- Gmail - 2024-11-19T18:15:00Z - gmail-repairs_maintenance-broad - "gmail-2026-02-14-hvac-invoice: Invoice from FastAir Engineering — emergency HVAC repair 14 Feb 2026. £886.30 inclusive of parts and emergency callout rate. CFO approved same day."

---

**Subscriptions | -£1,200 (-100.0%) | Budget: £1,200 | Actual: £0**

Actuals came in £1,200 below budget, representing a complete nil spend against a budgeted £1,200. No context was found across Slack, Gmail (broad and narrow searches), or any other source to explain this underspend. Possible explanations include: invoices not yet received or posted to the period; a subscription cancelled or not renewed; a contract renegotiation in progress; or a miscoding of the expense to another line. No context found — recommend review. Finance should confirm with the relevant budget holder whether subscriptions remain active, verify whether invoices are pending, and check whether any spend has been coded elsewhere in the period.

Sources:
- No sources found across Slack (broad and narrow) or Gmail (broad and narrow) for this line item in February 2026.

---

## INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES

The following line items produced variances that fall below the significance threshold and have not been investigated. They are noted here for completeness.

- **Sales** — £506 favourable (+8.4%)
- **Purchases** — -£500 adverse (-100.0%)
- **Audit & Accountancy Fees** — -£3 adverse (-6.1%)
- **Bank Fees** — -£15 favourable (-50.0%)
- **Cleaning** — -£1 favourable (-0.8%)
- **Entertainment - 0%** — -£50 favourable (-100.0%)
- **Entertainment - 100% Business** — £13 adverse (+0.0%)
- **General Expenses** — -£254 adverse (-211.7%)
- **Light Power Heating** — £13 adverse (+14.9%)
- **Motor Vehicle Expenses** — -£71 favourable (-54.6%)
- **Postage Freight Courier** — -£50 favourable (-100.0%)
- **Printing & Stationery** — -£20 favourable (-100.0%)
- **Rent** — -£46 favourable (-4.4%)
- **Telephone & Internet** — -£6 favourable (-13.3%)
- **Travel National** — -£150 favourable (-100.0%)

> **Note:** General Expenses shows a -211.7% variance, which may indicate a net credit or reversal in the period. While below the significance threshold on absolute value, it may warrant a brief ledger check if credits are unexpected.
